items.title: GTest and short-circuit evaluation in C++
items.description:
What is short-circuit evaluation in C++?

Short-circuit evaluation is a behavior of logical operators in C++ where the second part of an expression is evaluated only if it is necessary to determine the final result.

For example, in an expression using the logical OR operator, if

items.title: You Don't Own the Code AI Wrote for You
items.description:
AI is getting faster at generating HTML.

That sounds like good news. For most people reading this, it probably is.

items.title: I built a privacy-first JSON viewer for Chrome after I got tired of waiting
items.description:
A few months ago, like many devs, I noticed something off about the JSON viewer extension I'd been using for years.

items.title: Why We're Changing Our Default Eval Model
items.description: We're changing the default solver model in our eval harness from Claude Sonnet 4.6 to GLM 5.1. This is the default we provide to everyone running evals on the platform. items.title: offset-path

items.title: @function
items.description: The @function at-rule defines CSS custom functions.
